metadata.name: SAMA Cyber Security Framework
metadata.description: The SAMA Cyber Security Framework is a comprehensive regulatory framework issued by the Saudi Arabian Monetary Authority (SAMA) to ensure that financial institutions operating in Saudi Arabia maintain robust cybersecurity controls and practices.
metadata.classification_reasoning: The SAMA Cyber Security Framework is a mandatory compliance framework issued by the Saudi Arabian Monetary Authority for financial institutions operating in Saudi Arabia. It mandates specific cybersecurity controls and maturity levels, with enforcement by SAMA.
metadata.framework_purpose: To establish mandatory cybersecurity requirements for financial institutions in Saudi Arabia, ensuring the protection of critical financial infrastructure and customer data against cyber threats.
metadata.framework_scope: All financial institutions regulated by SAMA, including banks, insurance companies, finance companies, and other financial entities operating in Saudi Arabia.
framework_objectives: Establish a robust cybersecurity governance structure
Implement comprehensive cyber risk management practices
Ensure protection of critical information assets
Maintain cyber resilience and incident response capabilities
metadata.target_audience: CISOs, IT Security Teams, Compliance Officers, Risk Managers, and Board Members of SAMA-regulated financial institutions
metadata.penalty_for_non_compliance: Non-compliance may result in regulatory sanctions, fines, restrictions on business activities, and reputational damage as determined by SAMA.
adoption_approach: Conduct initial gap assessment against framework requirements
Develop remediation roadmap based on gap analysis
Implement required controls across all domains
Submit compliance reports to SAMA as required

sections: 3.2 Cyber Security Risk Management and Compliance
Appendix A - Overview previous issued SAMA circulars
Appendix B - How to request an Update to the Framework
Appendix C – Framework Update request form
Appendix D - How to request a Waiver from the Framework
Appendix E – Framework Waiver request form

main_requirement_sections: 3.2 Cyber Security Risk Management and Compliance

evidence_focus_areas: Compliance with international standards (e.g., NIST, ISO, PCI)

framework_classification.description: The SAMA Cyber Security Framework is a mandatory compliance framework issued by the Saudi Arabian Monetary Authority (SAMA) for financial institutions operating in Saudi Arabia. It mandates specific cybersecurity controls and maturity levels, with enforcement by SAMA.

controls.title: Adopt the Cyber Security Framework
controls.description: Member organizations are required to formally adopt the SAMA Cyber Security Framework as a guiding standard for their cybersecurity practices.
controls.full_text: The Member Organizations must adopt the Framework.
controls.ai_notes: The control explicitly states the requirement to adopt the framework, making it a directive control.

controls.title: Apply cyber security controls to applications and software
controls.description: Organizations must implement cybersecurity controls to protect applications, software, electronic services, and databases from unauthorized access or vulnerabilities.
controls.full_text: The Framework provides cyber security controls which are applicable to applications, software, electronic services, and databases.
controls.ai_notes: The control is preventive as it focuses on applying measures to avoid security incidents.

controls.title: Apply cyber security controls to computers and electronic machines
controls.description: Organizations must implement cybersecurity controls to secure computers and electronic machines, including ATMs, against threats.
controls.full_text: The Framework provides cyber security controls which are applicable to computers and electronic machines (e.g., ATM).
controls.ai_notes: The control is preventive as it focuses on securing devices to prevent incidents.

controls.title: Apply cyber security controls to electronic information
controls.description: Organizations must implement cybersecurity controls to protect electronic information from unauthorized access, alteration, or destruction.
controls.full_text: The Framework provides cyber security controls which are applicable to electronic information.
controls.ai_notes: The control is preventive as it focuses on protecting electronic information proactively.

controls.title: Apply cyber security controls to information assets
controls.description: Organizations must implement cybersecurity controls to safeguard their information assets from threats and vulnerabilities.
controls.full_text: The Framework provides cyber security controls which are applicable to the information assets of the Member Organization.
controls.ai_notes: The control is preventive as it focuses on protecting information assets proactively.

controls.title: Apply cyber security controls to information storage devices
controls.description: Organizations must implement cybersecurity controls to secure information storage devices, such as hard disks and USB sticks, against unauthorized access or data breaches.
controls.full_text: The Framework provides cyber security controls which are applicable to information storage devices (e.g., hard disk, USB stick).
controls.ai_notes: The control is preventive as it focuses on securing storage devices to prevent data breaches.

controls.title: Apply cyber security controls to physical information
controls.description: Organizations must implement cybersecurity controls to protect physical information, such as hardcopy documents, from unauthorized access or loss.
controls.full_text: The Framework provides cyber security controls which are applicable to physical information (hardcopy).
controls.ai_notes: The control is preventive as it focuses on protecting physical information from unauthorized access.

controls.title: Apply cyber security controls to premises, equipment, and communication networks
controls.description: Organizations must implement cybersecurity controls to secure their premises, equipment, and communication networks against physical and cyber threats.
controls.full_text: The Framework provides cyber security controls which are applicable to premises, equipment, and communication networks (technical infrastructure).
controls.ai_notes: The control is preventive as it focuses on securing infrastructure to prevent incidents.

controls.title: Implement cyber security controls
controls.description: Organizations must implement cybersecurity controls in alignment with the principles and objectives defined in the SAMA Cyber Security Framework.
controls.full_text: The Framework defines principles and objectives for implementing cyber security controls in Member Organizations.
controls.ai_notes: The control is directive as it provides guidance on implementing cybersecurity controls.

controls.title: Improve cyber security controls
controls.description: Organizations must continuously improve their cybersecurity controls in alignment with the principles and objectives defined in the SAMA Cyber Security Framework.
controls.full_text: The Framework defines principles and objectives for improving cyber security controls in Member Organizations.
controls.ai_notes: The control is corrective as it focuses on improving existing cybersecurity controls.

controls.title: Initiate cyber security controls
controls.description: Ensure that foundational cyber security controls are established to protect the organization from cyber threats.
controls.full_text: The Framework defines principles and objectives for initiating cyber security controls in Member Organizations.
controls.ai_notes: Clear directive to initiate controls.

controls.title: Maintain cyber security controls
controls.description: Ensure that existing cyber security controls are regularly reviewed and updated to remain effective.
controls.full_text: The Framework defines principles and objectives for maintaining cyber security controls in Member Organizations.
controls.ai_notes: Maintenance is explicitly required.

controls.title: Monitor cyber security controls
controls.description: Ensure that cyber security controls are continuously monitored to detect and respond to potential issues.
controls.full_text: The Framework defines principles and objectives for monitoring cyber security controls in Member Organizations.
controls.ai_notes: Monitoring is a key requirement.

controls.title: Provide direction for cyber security requirements for customers
controls.description: Establish and communicate cyber security requirements to customers to ensure their compliance and protection.
controls.full_text: The Framework provides direction for cyber security requirements for customers.
controls.ai_notes: Customer requirements are implied but not explicitly detailed.

controls.title: Provide direction for cyber security requirements for staff
controls.description: Define and enforce cyber security requirements for staff to ensure secure operations.
controls.full_text: The Framework provides direction for cyber security requirements for staff.
controls.ai_notes: Staff requirements are implied but not explicitly detailed.

controls.title: Provide direction for cyber security requirements for subsidiaries
controls.description: Ensure that subsidiaries comply with the organization's cyber security requirements.
controls.full_text: The Framework provides direction for cyber security requirements for Member Organizations and its subsidiaries.
controls.ai_notes: Subsidiary requirements are implied but not explicitly detailed.

controls.title: Provide direction for cyber security requirements for third parties
controls.description: Define and enforce cyber security requirements for third-party vendors to mitigate risks.
controls.full_text: The Framework provides direction for cyber security requirements for third parties.
controls.ai_notes: Third-party requirements are implied but not explicitly detailed.

controls.title: Adopt and implement the framework
controls.description: Ensure that the organization adopts and fully implements the SAMA Cyber Security Framework.
controls.full_text: The Member Organizations are responsible for adopting and implementing the Framework.
controls.ai_notes: Adoption and implementation are explicitly required.

controls.title: Mandate framework adoption by SAMA
controls.description: Ensure compliance with the mandatory adoption of the SAMA Cyber Security Framework.
controls.full_text: The framework is mandated by SAMA.
controls.ai_notes: Mandate is explicitly stated.

controls.title: Update the framework periodically
controls.description: Ensure that the framework is reviewed and updated periodically to address emerging threats and changes.
controls.full_text: SAMA is responsible for periodically updating the Framework.
controls.ai_notes: Periodic updates are explicitly required.

controls.title: Implement version control for the framework
controls.description: Ensure that changes to the framework are tracked and managed using version control to maintain integrity and traceability.
controls.full_text: Version control will be implemented for maintaining the Framework.

controls.title: Review the framework periodically
controls.description: Conduct regular reviews of the framework to ensure it remains effective and aligned with organizational and regulatory requirements.
controls.full_text: SAMA will review the Framework periodically to determine the Framework’s effectiveness.

controls.title: Submit framework update requests formally
controls.description: Member organizations should use a formal process to request updates to the framework when necessary.
controls.full_text: If a Member Organization considers that an update to the Framework is required, the Member Organization should formally submit the requested update to SAMA.
controls.ai_notes: The control is not mandatory, so evidence requirements are less stringent.

controls.title: Update the framework based on review outcomes
controls.description: Make necessary updates to the framework based on findings from periodic reviews to address gaps or changes in requirements.
controls.full_text: If applicable, SAMA will update the Framework based on the outcome of the review.

controls.title: Perform periodic self-assessment
controls.description: Conduct regular self-assessments to evaluate the organization's compliance with the framework and identify areas for improvement.
controls.full_text: The implementation of the Framework at the Member Organization will be subject to a periodic self-assessment.

controls.title: Perform self-assessment based on a questionnaire
controls.description: Use a standardized questionnaire to guide the self-assessment process and ensure consistency.
controls.full_text: The self-assessment will be performed by the Member Organization based on a questionnaire.

controls.title: SAMA to review and audit self-assessments
controls.description: SAMA will review and audit the self-assessments to verify compliance and assess the organization's cybersecurity maturity.
controls.full_text: The self-assessments will be reviewed and audited by SAMA to determine the level of compliance with the Framework and the cyber security maturity level of the Member Organization.

controls.title: Achieve preceding maturity levels before advancing
controls.description: Ensure that all criteria for lower maturity levels are met before progressing to higher levels.
controls.full_text: In order to achieve levels 3, 4 or 5, a Member Organization must first meet all criteria of the preceding maturity levels.

controls.title: Measure cyber security maturity level
controls.description: Use a predefined model to measure and evaluate the organization's cybersecurity maturity level.
controls.full_text: The cyber security maturity level will be measured with the help of a predefined cyber security maturity model.

controls.title: Define, approve, and implement cyber security controls
controls.description: Develop, formally approve, and implement cybersecurity controls to meet the requirements of maturity level 3.
controls.full_text: To achieve level 3 maturity, a Member Organization should define, approve and implement cyber security controls.
controls.ai_notes: The control is not mandatory, so evidence requirements are slightly relaxed.

controls.title: Ensure cyber security documentation includes 'why', 'what', and 'how'
controls.description: Ensure that cyber security documentation provides clear reasoning, scope, and implementation details for controls.
controls.full_text: The cyber security documentation should clearly indicate 'why', 'what' and 'how' cyber security controls should be implemented.
controls.ai_notes: The control is not mandatory but is critical for clarity in cyber security governance.

controls.title: Establish cyber security policies, standards, and procedures
controls.description: Develop and maintain comprehensive cyber security policies, standards, and procedures to guide organizational practices.
controls.full_text: The cyber security documentation consists of cyber security policies, cyber security standards and cyber security procedures.
controls.ai_notes: This is a mandatory control and foundational for governance.

controls.title: Monitor compliance with cyber security documentation
controls.description: Regularly monitor and assess adherence to established cyber security documentation to ensure compliance.
controls.full_text: To achieve level 3 maturity, a Member Organization should monitor compliance with the cyber security documentation.
controls.ai_notes: This control is tied to maturity level 3 and is not mandatory but highly recommended.

controls.title: Define key risk indicators (KRIs) for effectiveness measurement
controls.description: Identify and define key risk indicators (KRIs) to measure the effectiveness of cyber security controls.
controls.full_text: In order to measure and evaluate whether the cyber security controls are effective, key risk indicators (KRIs) should be defined.
controls.ai_notes: KRIs are essential for risk management but not explicitly mandatory in this framework.

controls.title: Periodically measure and evaluate effectiveness of controls
controls.description: Conduct regular evaluations to measure the effectiveness of implemented cyber security controls.
controls.full_text: To achieve maturity level 4, the Member Organization should periodically measure and evaluate the effectiveness of implemented cyber security controls.
controls.ai_notes: This control is tied to maturity level 4 and is critical for continuous improvement.

controls.title: Use KRIs for trend reporting and improvement identification
controls.description: Leverage KRIs to identify trends and areas for improvement in cyber security practices.
controls.full_text: KRIs are used for trend reporting and identification of potential improvements.
controls.ai_notes: This control supports proactive risk management but is not mandatory.

controls.title: Evaluate outsourcing policy and process
controls.description: Ensure that the organization's outsourcing policy and processes are regularly reviewed and assessed for effectiveness and compliance.
controls.full_text: Outsourcing policy and process should be measured and periodically evaluated.
controls.ai_notes: The control is not explicitly mandatory but is important for vendor management.

controls.title: Allocate sufficient budget for cyber security activities
controls.description: Ensure that the board allocates adequate financial resources to support all necessary cyber security initiatives.
controls.full_text: The board of the Member Organization should allocate sufficient budget to execute the required cyber security activities.
controls.ai_notes: The control is high priority but not explicitly mandatory.

controls.title: Appoint a full-time senior manager (CISO) for cyber security
controls.description: Ensure the appointment of a qualified, full-time CISO to oversee the organization's cyber security function.
controls.full_text: A full-time senior manager for the cyber security function, referred to as CISO, should be appointed at senior management level.
controls.ai_notes: The control is critical for governance but not explicitly mandatory.

controls.title: Develop and approve a cyber security committee charter
controls.description: Create and formally approve a charter that defines the purpose, structure, and responsibilities of the cyber security committee.
controls.full_text: A cyber security committee charter should be developed, approved and reflect committee objectives, roles and responsibilities, minimum number of meeting participants, and meeting frequency.
controls.ai_notes: The control is medium priority and supports governance.

controls.title: Ensure CISO has Saudi nationality and is qualified
controls.description: Verify that the appointed CISO is a Saudi national and possesses the necessary qualifications for the role.
controls.full_text: The Member Organization should ensure the CISO has a Saudi nationality and is sufficiently qualified.
controls.ai_notes: The control is culturally specific and medium priority.

controls.title: Ensure cyber security function is independent from IT
controls.description: Establish a cyber security function that operates independently from the IT department to avoid conflicts of interest.
controls.full_text: The cyber security function should be independent from the information technology function.
controls.ai_notes: The control is critical for governance and independence.

controls.title: Approve cyber security committee charter
controls.description: The Board of Directors must formally approve the charter that defines the roles, responsibilities, and authority of the cyber security committee.
controls.full_text: The Board of Directors has the ultimate responsibility for cyber security, including approving the cyber security committee charter.
controls.ai_notes: Clear directive for Board approval of the charter.

controls.title: Endorse cyber security governance
controls.description: The Board of Directors must formally endorse the cyber security governance framework after it has been approved by the cyber security committee.
controls.full_text: The Board of Directors has the ultimate responsibility for cyber security, including endorsing (after being approved by the cyber security committee) the cyber security governance.
controls.ai_notes: Directive is explicit about Board endorsement.

controls.title: Endorse cyber security strategy
controls.description: The Board of Directors must formally endorse the organization's cyber security strategy after it has been approved by the cyber security committee.
controls.full_text: The Board of Directors has the ultimate responsibility for cyber security, including endorsing (after being approved by the cyber security committee) the cyber security strategy.
controls.ai_notes: Directive is explicit about Board endorsement of the strategy.

controls.title: Endorse cyber security policy
controls.description: The Board of Directors must formally endorse the organization's cyber security policy after it has been approved by the cyber security committee.
controls.full_text: The Board of Directors has the ultimate responsibility for cyber security, including endorsing (after being approved by the cyber security committee) the cyber security policy.
controls.ai_notes: Directive is explicit about Board endorsement of the policy.

controls.title: Monitor, review, and communicate cyber security risk appetite
controls.description: The cyber security committee must ensure the organization's risk appetite is regularly reviewed, updated, and communicated to relevant stakeholders.
controls.full_text: The cyber security committee should be responsible for monitoring, reviewing and communicating the Member Organization’s cyber security risk appetite periodically or upon a material change in the risk appetite.
controls.ai_notes: Implicit requirement for periodic review and communication.

controls.title: Review cyber security strategy to align with objectives
controls.description: The cyber security committee must periodically review the cyber security strategy to ensure alignment with the organization's overall objectives.
controls.full_text: The cyber security committee should be responsible for reviewing the cyber security strategy to ensure that it supports the Member Organization objectives.
controls.ai_notes: Implicit requirement for periodic review.

controls.title: Approve and monitor cyber security risk management process
controls.description: The cyber security committee must oversee and ensure the effectiveness of the organization's risk management process for cyber security.
controls.full_text: The cyber security committee should be responsible for approving, communicating, supporting and monitoring the cyber security risk management process.
controls.ai_notes: The directive nature of this control is clear, but implementation details may vary by organization.

controls.title: Approve and monitor KRIs and KPIs for cyber security
controls.description: The cyber security committee must define, approve, and track KRIs and KPIs to measure and manage cyber security performance.
controls.full_text: The cyber security committee should be responsible for approving, communicating, supporting and monitoring the key risk indicators (KRIs) and key performance indicators (KPIs) for cyber security.
controls.ai_notes: The control is clear, but the frequency of monitoring may vary based on organizational needs.

controls.title: Ensure standards, processes, and procedures reflect security requirements
controls.description: Senior management must ensure that all organizational standards, processes, and procedures align with defined security requirements.
controls.full_text: The senior management should be responsible for ensuring that standards, processes and procedures reflect security requirements (if applicable).
controls.ai_notes: The control is preventive, but the scope of 'if applicable' may require clarification.

controls.title: Ensure compliance with cyber security policy and standards
controls.description: Senior management must ensure that all employees acknowledge and adhere to the organization's cyber security policies and standards.
controls.full_text: The senior management should be responsible for ensuring that individuals accept and comply with the cyber security policy, supporting standards and procedures when they are issued and updated.
controls.ai_notes: The control is clear, but the frequency of updates to policies may vary.

controls.title: Incorporate cyber security responsibilities in job descriptions
controls.description: Senior management must ensure that job descriptions for key roles include specific cyber security responsibilities.
controls.full_text: The senior management should be responsible for ensuring that cyber security responsibilities are incorporated in the job descriptions of key positions and cyber security staff.
controls.ai_notes: The control is preventive, but the scope of 'key positions' may require clarification.

controls.title: Develop and maintain cyber security strategy
controls.description: The CISO must create and regularly update a comprehensive cyber security strategy aligned with organizational goals.
controls.full_text: The CISO should be responsible for developing and maintaining the cyber security strategy.
controls.ai_notes: The control is clear, but the frequency of updates may vary based on organizational needs.

controls.title: Define security requirements for information assets
controls.description: Organizations must clearly define the security requirements for all information assets and related processes to ensure proper protection.
controls.full_text: Member Organizations have to ensure that security requirements for their information assets and the supporting processes are defined.
controls.ai_notes: Clear directive to define security requirements.

controls.title: Implement security requirements for information assets
controls.description: Organizations must ensure that the defined security requirements for information assets are put into practice.
controls.full_text: Member Organizations have to ensure that security requirements for their information assets and the supporting processes are implemented.
controls.ai_notes: Clear directive to implement security requirements.

controls.title: Measure and evaluate effectiveness of cyber security controls
controls.description: Organizations should periodically assess how well their cyber security controls are performing.
controls.full_text: The effectiveness of the cyber security controls should be periodically measured and evaluated.
controls.ai_notes: Periodic evaluation is recommended but not mandatory.

controls.title: Monitor compliance with cyber security requirements
controls.description: Organizations should monitor adherence to established cyber security requirements.
controls.full_text: The compliance with these cyber security requirements should be monitored.
controls.ai_notes: Monitoring is recommended but not explicitly mandatory.

controls.title: Approve cyber security requirements in HR processes
controls.description: HR processes should formally approve cyber security requirements to ensure alignment with organizational policies.
controls.full_text: The human resources process should approve cyber security requirements.
controls.ai_notes: Approval is implied but not explicitly mandatory.

controls.title: Define cyber security requirements in HR processes
controls.description: HR processes should define specific cyber security requirements relevant to their operations.
controls.full_text: The human resources process should define cyber security requirements.
controls.ai_notes: Definition is implied but not explicitly mandatory.

controls.title: Implement cyber security requirements in HR processes
controls.description: HR processes should ensure that defined cyber security requirements are implemented effectively.
controls.full_text: The human resources process should implement cyber security requirements.
controls.ai_notes: Implementation is implied but not explicitly mandatory.

controls.title: Measure and evaluate HR process effectiveness
controls.description: Organizations should periodically assess the effectiveness of HR processes in meeting cyber security requirements.
controls.full_text: The effectiveness of the human resources process should be measured and periodically evaluated.
controls.ai_notes: Evaluation is implied but not explicitly mandatory.

controls.title: Monitor effectiveness of HR processes
controls.description: Organizations should monitor the ongoing effectiveness of HR processes in meeting cyber security requirements.
controls.full_text: The effectiveness of the human resources process should be monitored.
controls.ai_notes: Monitoring is implied but not explicitly mandatory.

controls.title: Include cyber security responsibilities in staff agreements
controls.description: Staff agreements should include clauses outlining cyber security responsibilities and confidentiality obligations.
controls.full_text: The human resource process should include cyber security responsibilities and non-disclosure clauses within staff agreements (during and after the employment).
controls.ai_notes: Inclusion in agreements is implied but not explicitly mandatory.

controls.title: Provide cyber security awareness training to staff
controls.description: Ensure all staff are trained on cyber security awareness during onboarding and periodically throughout their employment.
controls.full_text: Staff should receive cyber security awareness at the start and during their employment.
controls.ai_notes: Training is implied as a best practice but not explicitly mandated.

controls.title: Define when disciplinary actions are applicable
controls.description: Establish clear guidelines on when disciplinary actions are applicable for cyber security violations.
controls.full_text: The human resource process should include when disciplinary actions will be applicable.
controls.ai_notes: Disciplinary actions are implied as part of HR processes but not explicitly required.

controls.title: Conduct screening and background checks
controls.description: Ensure all employees undergo screening and background checks as part of the hiring process.
controls.full_text: The human resource process should include screening and background check.
controls.ai_notes: Screening is a common HR practice but not explicitly mandated in the text.

controls.title: Revoke access rights post-employment
controls.description: Ensure all access rights are revoked immediately after an employee's termination.
controls.full_text: Post-employment cyber security activities should include revoking access rights.
controls.ai_notes: Revoking access is a critical security measure and is strongly implied.

controls.title: Ensure return of assigned information assets post-employment
controls.description: Ensure all assigned information assets are returned upon an employee's termination.
controls.full_text: Post-employment cyber security activities should include returning information assets assigned (e.g., access badge, tokens, mobile devices, all electronic and physical information).
controls.ai_notes: Returning assets is a standard practice but not explicitly mandated.

controls.title: Define, approve, implement, and monitor payment systems standard
controls.description: Establish and maintain a cyber security standard for payment systems, ensuring it is approved and monitored.
controls.full_text: The Member Organization should define, approve, implement and monitor a cyber security standard for payment systems.
controls.ai_notes: Defining and monitoring standards is a clear requirement.

controls.title: Measure and evaluate effectiveness of payment systems standard
controls.description: Regularly assess the effectiveness of the payment systems cyber security standard.
controls.full_text: The effectiveness of this process should be measured and periodically evaluated.
controls.ai_notes: Evaluation is implied as part of monitoring but not explicitly mandated.

controls.title: Define, approve, and implement electronic banking standards
controls.description: Establish and implement cyber security standards for electronic banking services.
controls.full_text: The cyber security standards for electronic banking services should be defined, approved and implemented.
controls.ai_notes: Defining and implementing standards is a clear requirement.

controls.title: Monitor compliance with electronic banking standards
controls.description: Ensure compliance with electronic banking standards is continuously monitored.
controls.full_text: The compliance with cyber security standards for electronic banking services should be monitored.
controls.ai_notes: Monitoring is implied as part of compliance but not explicitly mandated.

controls.title: Measure and evaluate effectiveness of electronic banking standards
controls.description: Regularly assess the effectiveness of the electronic banking cyber security standard.
controls.full_text: The effectiveness of the cyber security standard for electronic banking services should be measured and periodically evaluated.
controls.ai_notes: Evaluation is implied as part of monitoring but not explicitly mandated.

controls.title: Use brand protection measures for online services
controls.description: Ensure that online services, including social media, are protected from brand misuse or impersonation through appropriate measures.
controls.full_text: Electronic banking services security standard should cover the use of brand protection measures to protect online services including social media.
controls.ai_notes: The control is clear but does not explicitly mandate specific evidence types.

controls.title: Use official application stores and websites for online and mobile banking
controls.description: Ensure that online and mobile banking applications are only distributed through official application stores and websites to prevent unauthorized versions.
controls.full_text: Use of official application stores and websites (applicable for online and mobile banking).
controls.ai_notes: The control is explicit and aligns with preventive measures.

controls.title: Detect and take down malicious apps and websites
controls.description: Implement measures to detect and remove malicious apps and websites that could harm users or the organization.
controls.full_text: Use of detection measures and take-down of malicious apps and websites (applicable for online and mobile banking).
controls.ai_notes: The control is clear but does not specify the exact detection tools.

controls.title: Use sandboxing for online and mobile banking
controls.description: Implement sandboxing techniques to isolate and analyze potentially malicious activities in online and mobile banking environments.
controls.full_text: Use of sandboxing (applicable for online and mobile banking).
controls.ai_notes: The control is moderately clear but does not specify sandboxing tools or configurations.

controls.title: Use non-caching techniques for online and mobile banking
controls.description: Implement non-caching techniques to prevent sensitive data from being stored in browser or application caches.
controls.full_text: Use of non-caching techniques (applicable for online and mobile banking).
controls.ai_notes: The control is clear but does not specify implementation details.

controls.title: Use communication techniques to avoid man-in-the-middle attacks
controls.description: Implement secure communication techniques, such as encryption and certificate pinning, to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks.
controls.full_text: Use of communication techniques to avoid ‘man-in-the-middle’ attacks (applicable for online and mobile banking).
controls.ai_notes: The control is explicit and aligns with preventive measures.

controls.title: Implement physical security process
controls.description: Ensure physical security measures are in place to protect facilities and assets from unauthorized access or harm.
controls.full_text: The physical security process should be implemented.
controls.ai_notes: The control is clear but does not specify detailed implementation steps.

controls.title: Measure and evaluate physical security process effectiveness
controls.description: Regularly assess the performance of physical security measures to ensure they meet objectives and identify areas for improvement.
controls.full_text: The effectiveness of the physical security process should be measured and periodically evaluated.
controls.ai_notes: Evaluation frequency is implied but not explicitly stated.

controls.title: Monitor effectiveness of physical security process
controls.description: Continuously monitor physical security measures to detect and respond to potential weaknesses or breaches.
controls.full_text: The effectiveness of the physical security process should be monitored.
controls.ai_notes: Continuous monitoring is implied but not explicitly stated.

controls.title: Implement physical entry controls
controls.description: Establish controls to manage and restrict physical entry to facilities, including visitor management processes.
controls.full_text: The physical security process should include physical entry controls (including visitor security).
controls.ai_notes: Visitor security is explicitly mentioned, making the control clear.

controls.title: Implement monitoring and surveillance
controls.description: Deploy monitoring and surveillance systems to detect and deter unauthorized activities or breaches.
controls.full_text: The physical security process should include monitoring and surveillance (e.g., CCTV, ATMs GPS tracking, sensitivity sensors).
controls.ai_notes: Examples provided clarify the scope of monitoring and surveillance.

controls.title: Protect data centers and data rooms
controls.description: Implement physical security measures to safeguard data centers and rooms from unauthorized access or damage.
controls.full_text: The physical security process should include protection of data centers and data rooms.
controls.ai_notes: Data centers and rooms are explicitly mentioned, making the control clear.

controls.title: Define, implement, and monitor cloud security controls
controls.description: Establish and oversee cybersecurity controls for hybrid and public cloud environments to ensure secure operations.
controls.full_text: The Member Organization should define, implement and monitor the required cyber security controls within the cloud computing policy and process for hybrid and public cloud services.
controls.ai_notes: The control is clear and explicitly mentions hybrid and public cloud services.

controls.title: Measure and evaluate cloud security controls
controls.description: Assess the performance of cloud security controls to ensure they meet objectives and identify areas for improvement.
controls.full_text: The effectiveness of the defined cyber security controls should periodically be measured and evaluated.
controls.ai_notes: Evaluation frequency is implied but not explicitly stated.

controls.title: Obtain SAMA approval for material outsourcing
controls.description: Ensure SAMA approval is obtained before outsourcing critical functions or services.
controls.full_text: The outsourcing process should include the approval from SAMA prior to material outsourcing.
controls.ai_notes: SAMA approval is explicitly required, making the control clear.

controls.title: Involve cyber security function in outsourcing
controls.description: Ensure the cybersecurity team is engaged in the outsourcing process to address security risks.
controls.full_text: The outsourcing process should include the involvement of the cyber security function.
controls.ai_notes: Cybersecurity involvement is explicitly mentioned, making the control clear.

controls.title: Ensure compliance with SAMA outsourcing circular
controls.description: Ensure that all outsourcing activities comply with the requirements outlined in the SAMA outsourcing circular.
controls.full_text: The outsourcing process should include compliance with the SAMA circular on outsourcing.
controls.ai_notes: The control is directive in nature and requires evidence of compliance with SAMA's specific outsourcing guidelines.

controls.title: Define, approve, and implement secure disposal standard
controls.description: Establish and formalize a secure disposal standard to ensure proper handling of sensitive data during disposal.
controls.full_text: The secure disposal standard and procedure should be defined, approved and implemented.
controls.ai_notes: The control is preventive and foundational for secure disposal practices.

controls.title: Monitor compliance with secure disposal standard
controls.description: Regularly monitor adherence to the secure disposal standard to ensure compliance.
controls.full_text: The compliance with the secure disposal standard and procedure should be monitored.
controls.ai_notes: The control is detective and requires evidence of ongoing monitoring.

controls.title: Measure and evaluate effectiveness of secure disposal controls
controls.description: Assess the effectiveness of secure disposal controls to ensure they meet intended objectives.
controls.full_text: The effectiveness of the secure disposal cyber security controls should be measured and periodically evaluated.
controls.ai_notes: The control is corrective and focuses on periodic evaluation.

controls.title: Dispose of information assets per legal and regulatory requirements
controls.description: Ensure that information assets are disposed of in compliance with applicable legal and regulatory requirements.
controls.full_text: Information assets should be disposed in accordance with legal and regulatory requirements, when no longer required.
controls.ai_notes: The control is preventive and ensures legal compliance during disposal.

controls.title: Destroy sensitive information using non-retrievable techniques
controls.description: Use secure destruction methods to ensure sensitive information cannot be retrieved after disposal.
controls.full_text: Sensitive information should be destroyed using techniques to make the information non-retrievable (e.g., secure erase, secure wiping, incineration, double crosscut, shredding).
controls.ai_notes: The control is preventive and focuses on secure destruction methods.

controls.title: Ensure third-party compliance with secure disposal standards
controls.description: Verify that third-party vendors comply with secure disposal standards and procedures.
controls.full_text: The Member Organization should ensure that third party service providers used for secure disposal, transport and storage comply with the secure disposal standard and procedure.
controls.ai_notes: The control is directive and ensures third-party compliance.

controls.title: Measure and evaluate third-party secure disposal effectiveness
controls.description: Assess the effectiveness of third-party secure disposal processes to ensure compliance and adequacy.
controls.full_text: The effectiveness of third-party secure disposal processes should be periodically measured and evaluated.
controls.ai_notes: The control is corrective and focuses on periodic evaluation of third-party processes.

controls.title: Define, approve, implement, and communicate cloud security controls
controls.description: Establish and communicate cloud security controls to ensure secure use of hybrid and public cloud services.
controls.full_text: The cyber security controls within the cloud computing policy for hybrid and public cloud services should be defined, approved and implemented and communicated within Member Organization.
controls.ai_notes: The control is preventive and foundational for cloud security.

controls.title: Monitor compliance with cloud computing policy
controls.description: Regularly monitor adherence to the cloud computing policy to ensure compliance.
controls.full_text: The compliance with the cloud computing policy should be monitored.
controls.ai_notes: The control is detective and requires evidence of ongoing monitoring.

controls.title: Measure and evaluate cloud security controls periodically
controls.description: Ensure that cloud security controls are regularly reviewed and assessed to maintain their effectiveness and alignment with organizational policies.
controls.full_text: The cyber security controls regarding the cloud computing policy and process for hybrid and public cloud services should be periodically measured and evaluated.
controls.ai_notes: Periodic evaluation is implied but not explicitly mandated as a SHALL.

controls.title: Perform cyber security risk assessment for cloud services
controls.description: Conduct a thorough risk assessment and due diligence to identify potential security risks associated with the cloud service provider.
controls.full_text: A cyber security risk assessment and due diligence on the cloud service provider and its cloud services should be performed.
controls.ai_notes: The control explicitly requires a risk assessment and due diligence.

controls.title: Obtain SAMA approval before using cloud services
controls.description: Secure approval from SAMA before engaging with a cloud service provider or signing any contracts.
controls.full_text: The Member Organization should obtain SAMA approval prior to using cloud services or signing the contract with the cloud provider.
controls.ai_notes: Explicit requirement to obtain SAMA approval.

controls.title: Ensure contract includes cyber security requirements
controls.description: Ensure that the contract with the cloud provider explicitly includes all necessary cyber security requirements.
controls.full_text: A contract should be in place, including the cyber security requirements, before using cloud services.
controls.ai_notes: The control explicitly requires a contract with security requirements.

controls.title: Use cloud services located in Saudi Arabia or obtain SAMA approval
controls.description: Ensure cloud services are hosted within Saudi Arabia or obtain explicit approval from SAMA for services hosted outside the country.
controls.full_text: In principle only cloud services should be used that are located in Saudi Arabia, or when cloud services are to be used outside Saudi Arabia that the Member Organization should obtain explicit approval from SAMA.
controls.ai_notes: Explicit requirement to use Saudi-hosted services or obtain approval.

controls.title: Prohibit cloud provider from secondary use of data
controls.description: Ensure that the cloud provider is contractually prohibited from using organizational data for any purpose other than the agreed services.
controls.full_text: The cloud service provider should not use the Member Organization’s data for secondary purposes.
controls.ai_notes: Explicit prohibition of secondary data use is clear.

controls.title: Implement and monitor cloud security controls
controls.description: Ensure that the cloud provider implements and continuously monitors security controls to protect organizational data.
controls.full_text: The cloud service provider should implement and monitor the cyber security controls as determined in the risk assessment for protecting the confidentiality, integrity and availability of the Member Organization’s data.
controls.ai_notes: The control explicitly requires implementation and monitoring of security controls.

controls.title: Ensure logical segregation of data in cloud services
controls.description: Ensure that the cloud provider logically separates organizational data from other clients' data to maintain data integrity and confidentiality.
controls.full_text: The Member Organization’s data is logically segregated from other data held by the cloud service provider, including that the cloud service provider should be able to identify the Member Organization’s data and at all times should be able to distinguish it from other data.
controls.ai_notes: Logical segregation is explicitly required.

controls.title: Meet business continuity requirements for cloud services
controls.description: Ensure that the cloud provider's services align with the organization's business continuity requirements to minimize disruptions.
controls.full_text: Business continuity requirements are met in accordance with the Member Organization’s business continuity policy.
controls.ai_notes: Business continuity alignment is explicitly required.

controls.title: Perform cyber security review at cloud provider
controls.description: Ensure the organization exercises its right to conduct periodic cyber security reviews of the cloud provider.
controls.full_text: The Member Organization has the right to perform a cyber security review at the cloud service provider.
controls.ai_notes: The control implies periodic reviews but does not specify frequency.

controls.title: Perform cyber security audit at cloud provider
controls.description: The organization should ensure it has the ability to conduct audits of the cloud provider's cybersecurity practices to verify compliance with agreed standards.
controls.full_text: The Member Organization has the right to perform a cyber security audit at the cloud service provider.
controls.ai_notes: The control is clear but not mandatory, so evidence should focus on optional implementation.

controls.title: Perform cyber security examination at cloud provider
controls.description: The organization should ensure it can conduct examinations of the cloud provider's cybersecurity measures to assess risks and compliance.
controls.full_text: The Member Organization has the right to perform a cyber security examination at the cloud service provider.
controls.ai_notes: Similar to audits, but examinations may be less formal and more risk-focused.

controls.title: Ensure termination rights in cloud contracts
controls.description: The organization should ensure that its contract with the cloud provider includes clear termination rights to protect its interests.
controls.full_text: The Member Organization has termination rights.
controls.ai_notes: Termination rights are critical but may vary based on jurisdiction and contract terms.

controls.title: Ensure data return upon termination
controls.description: The organization should ensure that the cloud provider is contractually obligated to return all data upon termination of the agreement.
controls.full_text: The cloud service provider has to return the Member Organization’s data on termination.
controls.ai_notes: High priority due to the criticality of data ownership and retrieval.

controls.title: Ensure irreversible data deletion upon termination
controls.description: The organization should ensure that the cloud provider is contractually obligated to irreversibly delete all data upon termination to prevent unauthorized access.
controls.full_text: The cloud service provider has to irreversibly delete the Member Organization’s data on termination.
controls.ai_notes: High priority due to the risk of data breaches if deletion is not properly executed.

controls.title: Establish a cyber security awareness program
controls.description: The organization should implement a program to educate employees on cybersecurity best practices and policies.
controls.full_text: A program that explains proper rules of behavior for the safe and secure use of IT systems and information. The program communicates cyber security policies and procedures that need to be followed.
controls.ai_notes: Mandatory control with clear evidence requirements.

controls.title: Establish an independent cyber security function
controls.description: The organization should establish a dedicated cybersecurity function led by a CISO to ensure independence and effective governance.
controls.full_text: A function, independent from the information technology function, that is headed by a CISO and that reports directly to the CEO/managing director of the Member Organization or general manager of a control function.
controls.ai_notes: Mandatory control with clear governance requirements.

controls.title: Ascertain that cyber risks are managed appropriately
controls.description: The organization should ensure that cyber risks are identified, assessed, and mitigated effectively.
controls.full_text: Ascertaining that cyber risks are managed appropriately.
controls.ai_notes: Mandatory control with clear risk management requirements.

controls.title: Ensure cyber security objectives are achieved
controls.description: The organization should monitor and measure progress toward achieving its cybersecurity objectives.
controls.full_text: Ensuring that cyber security objectives are achieved.
controls.ai_notes: Mandatory control with clear focus on objective tracking.

controls.title: Provide strategic direction for cyber security
controls.description: The organization should define and communicate a strategic plan for its cybersecurity initiatives.
controls.full_text: Providing strategic direction for cyber security.
controls.ai_notes: Mandatory control with a focus on strategic alignment.

controls.title: Verify responsible use of enterprise resources
controls.description: Ensure that enterprise resources, such as IT systems, data, and personnel, are utilized in a manner that aligns with organizational policies and objectives.
controls.full_text: Verifying that the enterprise's resources are used responsibly.
controls.ai_notes: The control is explicitly stated but requires interpretation for evidence specifics.

controls.title: Conduct a risk assessment
controls.description: Perform a comprehensive evaluation of potential risks to the organization's information systems and data.
controls.full_text: Consists of (i) a risk assessment; (ii) the implementation of a risk mitigation strategy; and (iii) employment of techniques and procedures for the continuous monitoring of the security state of the information system.
controls.ai_notes: The control is clearly defined with explicit requirements.

controls.title: Employ techniques for continuous monitoring of security state
controls.description: Implement tools and processes to continuously monitor the security posture of the organization's information systems.
controls.full_text: Employment of techniques and procedures for the continuous monitoring of the security state of the information system.
controls.ai_notes: The control is explicitly stated and aligns with standard monitoring practices.

controls.title: Implement a risk mitigation strategy
controls.description: Develop and execute a strategy to reduce identified risks to an acceptable level.
controls.full_text: Implementation of a risk mitigation strategy.
controls.ai_notes: The control is clearly defined and requires evidence of implementation.

controls.title: Alert operational management to emerging information security issues
controls.description: Notify operational management of new or evolving security threats and regulatory changes that may impact the organization.
controls.full_text: Alerting operational management to emerging information security issues and changing regulatory and information risk scenarios.
controls.ai_notes: The control is well-defined but may require clarification on notification timelines.

controls.title: Assist management in developing information security processes and controls
controls.description: Support management in creating and refining processes and controls to address information security risks.
controls.full_text: Assisting management in developing information security processes and controls to manage information security risks and information security issues.
controls.ai_notes: The control is clear but may require additional context for evidence specifics.

controls.title: Define information security roles and responsibilities
controls.description: Clearly define and document roles and responsibilities for information security within the organization.
controls.full_text: Defining information security roles and responsibilities.
controls.ai_notes: The control is explicit and requires clear documentation.

controls.title: Facilitate and monitor implementation of effective information security practices
controls.description: Oversee and track the adoption of information security practices by operational teams to ensure effectiveness.
controls.full_text: Facilitating and monitoring implementation of effective information security and information risk management practices by operational management.
controls.ai_notes: The control is well-defined but may require clarification on monitoring scope.

controls.title: Identify known and emerging information security issues
controls.description: Continuously identify and document both existing and emerging security threats to the organization.
controls.full_text: Identifying known and emerging information security issues.
controls.ai_notes: The control is explicit and aligns with standard threat identification practices.

controls.title: Identify shifts in the organization's implicit information risk appetite
controls.description: Monitor and document changes in the organization's tolerance for information security risks.
controls.full_text: Identifying shifts in the organizations implicit information risk appetite.
controls.ai_notes: The control is somewhat implicit and may require additional clarification for evidence specifics.

controls.title: Monitor adequacy and effectiveness of internal controls
controls.description: Ensure that internal controls related to information security are effective, accurate, and compliant with regulations, and address any deficiencies promptly.
controls.full_text: Monitoring the adequacy and effectiveness of internal control, accuracy and completeness of reporting, compliance with laws and regulations in connection with information security, and timely remediation of deficiencies.
controls.ai_notes: This control is explicitly stated as mandatory and critical for compliance.

controls.title: Provide guidance and training on information security and risk management
controls.description: Deliver training and guidance to employees on information security and risk management to ensure awareness and compliance.
controls.full_text: Providing guidance and training on information security and information risk management processes.
controls.ai_notes: Training is a critical component of compliance and is explicitly required.

controls.title: Provide information security and risk management frameworks
controls.description: Develop and provide structured frameworks for managing information security and associated risks.
controls.full_text: Providing information security and information risk management frameworks.
controls.ai_notes: Frameworks are foundational to governance and explicitly required.

controls.title: Set information security goals for implementation
controls.description: Define clear and measurable goals for implementing information security initiatives.
controls.full_text: Setting information security goals for implementation.
controls.ai_notes: Goal setting is explicitly required and critical for strategic planning.

controls.title: Support information security policies
controls.description: Ensure that information security policies are supported, roles and responsibilities are defined, and goals are established.
controls.full_text: Supporting information security policies, defining information security roles and responsibilities, and setting information security goals for implementation.
controls.ai_notes: Policy support and role definition are explicitly required.

controls.title: Adopt secure coding standards
controls.description: Implement secure coding standards to minimize the risk of introducing vulnerabilities during software development.
controls.full_text: A secure coding standard describes a uniform set of rules and guidelines for developing computer software that protects against the accidental introduction of security vulnerabilities.
controls.ai_notes: Secure coding is explicitly required to prevent vulnerabilities.

controls.title: Classify sensitive information
controls.description: Classify sensitive information based on the organization's data classification policy to ensure appropriate handling and protection.
controls.full_text: Sensitive information is the information deemed sensitive according to the organizational data classification policy.
controls.ai_notes: Data classification is explicitly required for compliance.

controls.title: Conduct periodic security assessments for vendors
controls.description: Perform regular security assessments of vendors to ensure they meet the organization's information security standards.
controls.full_text: Vendor management includes periodic security assessments to ensure third-party service providers adhere to the same information security standards that an organization must comply with.
controls.ai_notes: Vendor assessments are explicitly required for compliance.

controls.title: Conduct root-cause analysis for risks
controls.description: Identify the underlying causes of risks to address them effectively and prevent recurrence.
controls.full_text: Root-cause analysis is a principle-based, systems approach for the identification of underlying causes associated with a particular set of risks.
controls.ai_notes: Root-cause analysis is implied as necessary for effective risk management.

controls.title: Define service level agreements (SLAs)
controls.description: Establish SLAs with service providers to define responsibilities and set clear expectations.
controls.full_text: A service level agreement (SLA) defines the specific responsibilities of the service provider and sets the customer expectations.
controls.ai_notes: SLAs are explicitly required for vendor management.

controls.title: Ensure physical security of facilities
controls.description: Ensure that physical locations housing critical information assets are protected against unauthorized access, damage, or interference.
controls.full_text: The physical protection of facilities that host information assets against intentional and unintentional security events.
controls.ai_notes: Physical security is explicitly required to protect facilities hosting information assets.

controls.title: Ensure secure disposal of equipment and media
controls.description: Ensure that sensitive data on equipment and media is securely erased or destroyed before disposal to prevent unauthorized access.
controls.full_text: Secure disposal minimizes the risk of unwanted disclosure. See also 'Secure erase', 'Secure wiping', 'Incineration', and 'Double crosscut'.
controls.ai_notes: Secure disposal is a critical control to prevent data leakage.

controls.title: Establish a risk register
controls.description: Maintain a centralized repository to document, track, and manage identified risks and their mitigation plans.
controls.full_text: Risk register is a table used as a repository for all risks identified and includes additional information about each risk, e.g. risk category, risk owner, and mitigation actions taken.
controls.ai_notes: Risk register is a foundational element of risk management.

controls.title: Establish a security operations center (SOC)
controls.description: Set up a dedicated team and infrastructure to monitor, detect, and respond to security incidents in real-time.
controls.full_text: A security operations center (SOC) is a specialized location (and team) where security-related data from enterprise information systems is monitored, assessed and actioned.
controls.ai_notes: SOC is a critical component for real-time security monitoring and response.

controls.title: Implement red-teaming exercises
controls.description: Conduct simulated attacks to test the organization's ability to detect, respond to, and recover from security incidents.
controls.full_text: Red-teaming is an exercise, reflecting real-world conditions, that is conducted as a simulated adversarial attempt to compromise organizational missions and/or business processes to provide a comprehensive assessment of the security capability of the information system and organization.
controls.ai_notes: Red-teaming is explicitly mentioned as a critical testing mechanism.

controls.title: Implement risk treatment processes
controls.description: Develop and implement strategies to address identified risks, including mitigation, transfer, or acceptance.
controls.full_text: Risk treatment is a process to modify risk that can involve avoiding the risk by deciding not to start or continue with the activity that gives rise to the risk; taking or increasing risk in order to pursue an opportunity; removing the risk source; changing the likelihood; changing the consequences; sharing the risk with another party or parties; and retaining the risk by informed decision.
controls.ai_notes: Risk treatment is a core component of risk management.

controls.title: Implement sandboxing for controlled execution
controls.description: Use sandboxing to isolate and analyze potentially malicious software in a controlled environment.
controls.full_text: Sandboxing is a restricted, controlled execution environment that prevents potentially malicious software, such as mobile code, from accessing any system resources except those for which the software is authorized.
controls.ai_notes: Sandboxing is a well-defined preventive control for malware protection.

controls.title: Implement SIEM tools for security data gathering
controls.description: Deploy SIEM tools to collect, analyze, and correlate security data for real-time monitoring and incident response.
controls.full_text: A security information and event management (SIEM) tool provides the ability to gather security data from information system components and presents that data as actionable information via a single interface.
controls.ai_notes: SIEM tools are explicitly required for centralized security monitoring.

controls.title: Implement vulnerability management practices
controls.description: Establish a process to regularly identify, assess, and remediate vulnerabilities in the organization's systems and applications.
controls.full_text: Vulnerability management is the cyclical practice of identifying, classifying, remediating, and mitigating vulnerabilities.
controls.ai_notes: Vulnerability management is a critical security practice.

controls.title: Maintain resilience under adverse conditions
controls.description: Ensure the organization can continue critical operations during and after adverse events, such as cyberattacks or natural disasters.
controls.full_text: The ability to continue to operate under adverse conditions or stress, even if in a degraded or debilitated state, while maintaining essential operational capabilities.
controls.ai_notes: Resilience is a key requirement for business continuity.

controls.title: Mitigate negative consequences of risks
controls.description: This control ensures that risks with negative consequences are addressed through appropriate mitigation strategies to reduce their impact.
controls.full_text: Risk treatments that deal with negative consequences are sometimes referred to as 'risk mitigation', 'risk elimination', 'risk prevention' and 'risk reduction'.
controls.ai_notes: Clear directive to mitigate risks with negative consequences.

controls.title: Perform security testing of information systems
controls.description: This control ensures that security testing is conducted to evaluate the effectiveness of safeguards protecting information systems.
controls.full_text: Security testing involves examination and analysis of the safeguards required to protect an information system, as they have been applied in an operational environment, to determine the security posture of that system.
controls.ai_notes: Security testing is explicitly required to determine system security posture.

controls.title: Promote a risk-aware culture
controls.description: This control ensures that the organization fosters a culture where employees are aware of and take responsibility for managing risks.
controls.full_text: A strong risk-aware culture involves shared values, beliefs, knowledge, attitudes and understanding about risk within an organization. In a strong risk culture, people proactively identify, discuss and take responsibility for risks.
controls.ai_notes: Promoting a risk-aware culture is implied but not explicitly mandated.

controls.title: Recover to effective operational posture
controls.description: This control ensures that the organization can recover operations effectively within a timeframe that supports its mission.
controls.full_text: Recover to an effective operational posture in a time frame consistent with mission needs.
controls.ai_notes: Clear directive to recover operations within mission-aligned timeframes.

controls.title: Use scrubbing services to analyze and remove malicious traffic
controls.description: This control ensures that scrubbing services are used to filter out malicious traffic from the organization's network.
controls.full_text: Scrubbing services analyze an organization's network traffic and remove malicious traffic (DDoS, known vulnerabilities and exploits).
controls.ai_notes: Scrubbing services are explicitly required to remove malicious traffic.

controls.title: Action security-related data in SOC
controls.description: This control ensures that the SOC takes appropriate actions based on security-related data from enterprise systems.
controls.full_text: The SOC shall action security-related data from enterprise information systems (e.g., web sites, applications, databases, servers, networks, desktops, and other devices).
controls.ai_notes: SOC actions on security-related data are explicitly required.

controls.title: Assess security-related data in SOC
controls.description: This control ensures that the SOC evaluates security-related data to identify potential threats or vulnerabilities.
controls.full_text: The SOC shall assess security-related data from enterprise information systems (e.g., web sites, applications, databases, servers, networks, desktops, and other devices).
controls.ai_notes: SOC assessment of security-related data is explicitly required.

controls.title: Detect indicators of compromise in SOC
controls.description: This control ensures that the SOC is focused on identifying indicators of compromise to detect potential security incidents.
controls.full_text: The SOC shall be dedicated to the detection of indicators of compromise.
controls.ai_notes: Detection of indicators of compromise is explicitly required.

controls.title: Disseminate security-related information from SOC
controls.description: This control ensures that the SOC shares security-related information with relevant stakeholders within the organization.
controls.full_text: The SOC shall disseminate collated security-related information to other areas of the organization (e.g., the cyber security function, incident management team, and IT service owners).
controls.ai_notes: Dissemination of security-related information is explicitly required.

controls.title: Investigate indicators of compromise in SOC
controls.description: This control ensures that the SOC investigates indicators of compromise to determine the nature and scope of potential security incidents.
controls.full_text: The SOC shall be dedicated to the investigation of indicators of compromise.
controls.ai_notes: Investigation of indicators of compromise is explicitly required.

controls.title: Monitor security-related data in SOC
controls.description: The Security Operations Center (SOC) must continuously monitor security-related data from all enterprise systems to detect potential threats or anomalies.
controls.full_text: The SOC shall monitor security-related data from enterprise information systems (e.g., web sites, applications, databases, servers, networks, desktops, and other devices).

controls.title: Respond to indicators of compromise in SOC
controls.description: The SOC must be prepared to respond to any indicators of compromise (IoCs) to mitigate potential security incidents.
controls.full_text: The SOC shall be dedicated to the potential response to indicators of compromise.

controls.title: Collect information on threats in a specific domain
controls.description: Organizations must gather detailed information about threats, risks, and vulnerabilities specific to their operational domain.
controls.full_text: The threat landscape shall include a collection of threats in a particular domain or context, with information on identified vulnerable assets, threats, risks, threat actors, and observed trends.

controls.title: Provide an overview of threats and trends
controls.description: Organizations must maintain an up-to-date overview of current and emerging threats and trends to inform risk management strategies.
controls.full_text: The threat landscape shall include an overview of threats, together with current and emerging trends.

controls.title: Conduct periodic security assessments of third-party providers
controls.description: Organizations must regularly assess the security posture of third-party service providers to ensure compliance with security requirements.
controls.full_text: Vendor management shall include periodic security assessments of third-party service providers.

controls.title: Ensure third-party providers adhere to security standards
controls.description: Organizations must ensure that third-party providers comply with the same security standards and requirements as the organization itself.
controls.full_text: Vendor management shall ensure that third-party service providers adhere to the same information security standards that an organization must comply with.

controls.title: Classify vulnerabilities
controls.description: Organizations must classify identified vulnerabilities based on their severity and potential impact.
controls.full_text: Vulnerability management shall include the classification of vulnerabilities.

controls.title: Identify vulnerabilities
controls.description: Organizations must identify vulnerabilities in their systems, applications, and networks to address potential risks.
controls.full_text: Vulnerability management shall include the identification of vulnerabilities.

controls.title: Mitigate vulnerabilities
controls.description: Organizations must implement measures to mitigate identified vulnerabilities and reduce associated risks.
controls.full_text: Vulnerability management shall include the mitigation of vulnerabilities.

controls.title: Remediate vulnerabilities
controls.description: Organizations must remediate vulnerabilities by applying fixes or patches to eliminate risks.
controls.full_text: Vulnerability management shall include the remediation of vulnerabilities.
